intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: make planning and Memory cooperate tightly at the workflow level so work pulls relevant durable understanding before execution and preserves later-useful residue after finishing, while keeping both modules independently usable.

required_continuation.activation trigger: follow the planning-owned continuation recorded in the startup-planning-surface-health archive; any remaining open follow-on after that handoff now lives in .agentic-workspace/planning/state.toml under the separate memory-recurring-friction-improvement-pressure lane.

intent_interpretation.literal request: continue and finish the active lane, and commit after each slice
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: keep advancing the top-priority planning-memory lane through honest bounded slices, leaving checked-in proof and a clean commit after each completed tranche
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: activate `#266` next and add the compact post-work posterity question to the closure path before deciding whether `#262` is still needed
intent_interpretation.review guidance: reject the slice if it turns the prompt into a Memory dependency, a broad note-taking ritual, or a substitute for the still-open startup-drift question.

execution_bounds.required validation commands: `uv run pytest packages/planning/tests/test_installer.py -q`; `uv run pytest packages/planning/tests/test_check_planning_surfaces.py -q`; `uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py`; `uv run agentic-workspace summary --format json`
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: stop before broad summary/report redesign or any memory-module implementation change beyond optional destination wording
execution_bounds.stop before touching: unrelated graceful-compliance, portability, machine-first planning, or memory-package-owned accumulation workflows

stop_conditions.stop when: the smallest honest host surface for the post-work question would require a new planning schema family or a hard dependency on Memory
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: the posterity prompt cannot be made useful without redesigning summary/report/handoff contracts more broadly than this bounded slice
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: more than the nearest planning-owned closure surfaces, docs, tests, and narrow reporting proof would need reshaping
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: the slice cannot show that ordinary closure now asks what should survive and where it belongs without adding more ceremony than value

context_budget.live working set: issue `#266`, the existing execution-summary closure path, archive validation rules, and the delegated handoff return contract
context_budget.recoverable later: broader lane rationale from `#264`, startup drift work from `#262`, and recurring-friction accumulation expectations from `#263`
context_budget.externalize before shift: the chosen host field, the exact posterity question, and the rule that planning-first closure must still work without Memory installed
context_budget.tiny resumability note: keep the post-work prompt about compact posterity capture and routing, not broad retrospection
context_budget.context-shift triggers: shift once the host surface is chosen or if the posterity cue requires broader reporting or memory-ownership redesign

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: land the smallest useful post-work posterity prompt in the planning chain and prove it is cheap enough for routine closure use
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: keep the change compact, planning-owned, closure-focused, and explicitly non-dependent on Memory
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: the exact field wording, the narrowest host surface, and the smallest proof that delegated or direct work will actually see the prompt at finish time
delegated_judgment.escalate when: the change would require a broader planning-memory architecture pass, a new closure subsystem, or memory-owned implementation work
